task_completion.description: Verify that the populated places input layer is loaded into the GIS software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layer 'ne_110m_populated_places' is visible in the Table of Contents (Layers panel) on the left side of the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opened the Voronoi or Thiessen Polygons geoprocessing tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or panel for generating 'Voronoi' or 'Thiessen Polygons' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the tool is configured with the correct input layer and output file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the geoprocessing tool dialog, the input layer is set to 'ne_110m_populated_places' and the output file path is explicitly set to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/voronoi_cities.shp'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the resulting Voronoi polygons layer is generated and added to the View.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new polygon layer (e.g., 'voronoi_cities') appears in the Table of Contents, and the main map view displays a tessellation of polygons (a network of intersecting lines forming cells around the points).

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the GIS graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the GIS software's menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes rather than executing a script or command-line tool (like ogr2ogr or python) in a terminal window.

integrity.description: Verify that the GIS software genuinely rendered the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map view shows actual Voronoi polygons corresponding to the point distribution, indicating the software successfully processed the geometry, rather than just an empty layer or a renamed file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the application using terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows open where the agent is running automated scripts or GDAL commands to generate the shapefile.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input dataset contains exactly 243 points. A correct Voronoi tessellation will yield exactly 243 polygons (acceptable range 200-280). The output must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/voronoi_cities.shp' and visually appear as a polygon tessellation covering the points.
